name: A4 family symmetry
summary: Discrete non-abelian flavor symmetry A_4 (the tetrahedral group) under which the three lepton families are arranged in a 3-dimensional irreducible representation. Particular vacuum alignments of A_4-charged flavons reproduce tribimaximal or related PMNS mixing patterns close to observation.

addresses_puzzles.note: Combined with seesaw, A_4 produces near-tribimaximal PMNS structure with corrections from flavon VEV alignment.

touches_benchmarks.note: Tribimaximal-like predictions: theta_12 ~ 35.3 degrees (sin^2 theta_12 = 1/3), theta_23 maximal, theta_13 = 0 at LO; corrections from charged-lepton sector and higher-order operators bring theta_13 to observed ~9 degrees.
A_4-induced charged-lepton flavor violation can saturate MEG bounds depending on flavon scale.

introduces.note: Multiple flavon VEVs and alignment parameters; vacuum alignment is the central technical challenge.

history: Ma-Rajasekaran (2001) introduced A_4 for lepton mixing. Tribimaximal mixing (Harrison-Perkins-Scott 2002) was a target prediction; non-zero theta_13 measured by Daya Bay/RENO/Double Chooz (2012) required corrections. Many variants (S_4, A_5, Delta(27)) explored.

references.citation: Ma and Rajasekaran, 'Softly broken A_4 symmetry for nearly degenerate neutrino masses', Phys. Rev. D 64, 113012 (2001)
Altarelli and Feruglio, 'Discrete flavor symmetries and models of neutrino mixing', Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 2701 (2010)
